questions.question: Write the VHDL entity and architecture for a full adder. Explain each line. (5 marks)
keyPoints: Entity declaration: entity FullAdder with ports A, B, Cin (in bit) and S, Cout (out bit).
Architecture body: S <= A xor B xor Cin (sum is 3-input XOR).
Cout <= (A and B) or (A and Cin) or (B and Cin) (carry is majority function).
Entity defines the interface; architecture defines the behavior.

questions.question: Design a 4-bit ripple-carry adder using Verilog. Show the full adder module and the top-level 4-bit adder module using structural instantiation or generate statements. Explain how carry propagation works. (10 marks)
keyPoints: Full adder module: inputs A, B, Cin; outputs S, Cout; S = A ^ B ^ Cin; Cout = (A&B)|(A&Cin)|(B&Cin).
4-bit adder: instantiate 4 full adders FA0 through FA3.
Carry chain: Cout of FA0 connects to Cin of FA1, Cout of FA1 to Cin of FA2, etc.
FA0 receives the external carry-in; FA3's Cout is the final carry-out.
Generate statement can be used for scalability: generate for(i=0; i<4; i=i+1).
Carry ripples from LSB to MSB, hence the name ripple-carry adder. The critical path delay is proportional to the number of bits.
